title: Today's AI Brief: 9 Demos of Gemini Omni and Gemini 3.5 in Action
summary: Google showcases 9 demos of Gemini Omni and Gemini 3.5, highlighting AI advancements.
imageAlt: Image from source article for 9 demos of Gemini Omni and Gemini 3.5 in action
imageCaption: 9 demos of Gemini Omni and Gemini 3.5 in action
imageSourceTitle: 9 demos of Gemini Omni and Gemini 3.5 in action

body.text: The AI world is rapidly evolving, with major players like Google pushing the boundaries of what's possible. I've been checking out the latest developments from Google, and it's clear that their Gemini models are making waves. The company just released 9 demos showcasing Gemini Omni and Gemini 3.5 in action, and it's impressive to see these AI models in practice. What caught my attention is how these models are being applied in real-world scenarios, from generating music to creating chatbots.

The demos themselves are quite revealing. With Gemini Omni, Google is combining the ability to reason with the ability to create. Meanwhile, Gemini 3.5 is designed to help users generate content and solve problems. I'm intrigued by the potential applications of these models, and it's exciting to think about where this technology could go. For instance, imagine being able to generate music or art that's not only creative but also tailored to your specific needs.

In related news, Google has created a fun quiz in Google AI Studio that tests your knowledge of their I/O 2026 announcements. The quiz, which they've dubbed 'vibe coded,' is a lighthearted way to engage with the company's latest AI developments. I have to admit, I'm curious to see how well I can do - it's like a game of AI trivia. And if you're a developer or just an AI enthusiast, it's definitely worth checking out.

My quick read: the artificial intelligence story keeps getting bigger, but also stranger in very human ways. One moment it is strategy and infrastructure; the next, the Vatican is in the room. That is a pretty good sign this technology has left the lab and wandered into the town square.
